mechanistic_narrative: Endothelial self-clustering reflects vascular network topology. CD31+/CD34+ endothelial cells in the peritubular capillary plexus are physically connected. PECAM1 mediates homophilic adhesion (GO:0007156). Both share glomerular endothelium development (GO:0072011).

biological_context: High-belief complex despite single evidence
shared_go_processes: positive regulation of cell migration (GO:0030335); protein homodimerization activity (GO:0042803); positive regulation of ERK1 and ERK2 cascade (GO:0070374); cell migration (GO:0016477)
mechanistic_narrative: Endothelial-fibroblast proximity reflects the vascular-interstitial interface. PDGFRB on pericytes physically contacts endothelial cells. PDGFRA/PECAM1 share cell migration regulation (GO:0030335). In AKI, this interface is reported to be where capillary rarefaction and fibrosis originate.

biological_context: CD11b-PECAM1 complex — transendothelial migration
shared_go_processes: leukocyte cell-cell adhesion (GO:0007159); cell-cell adhesion (GO:0098609)
mechanistic_narrative: Endothelial-macrophage proximity reflects leukocyte extravasation. PECAM1 mediates diapedesis (GO:0050904) and neutrophil extravasation (GO:0072672). ITGAM on myeloid cells adheres to PECAM1 via leukocyte cell-cell adhesion (GO:0007159). This is the transendothelial migration axis.
